description_long: This cowshed is a rectangular, single-story structure with a prominent hip roof clad in red corrugated material. The walls are constructed from horizontal wooden planks, giving it a rustic and sturdy appearance. The front features a large sliding barn door with visible black metal hinges and a partial open section supported by wooden posts, likely serving as a sheltered entry or feeding area. The roof overhangs the walls, providing additional protection from the elements. There are no visible windows, and the structure is elevated slightly above the ground on concrete footings. The overall design is functional, with a focus on durability and shelter for livestock.
description: A rectangular wooden cowshed with a red hip roof, large sliding barn door, and open sheltered entry supported by posts. The structure is rustic and functional, with no visible windows.
description_view_0: Front view showing the large sliding barn door, black metal hinges, and an open entry area supported by posts.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the open sheltered section and the hip roof's overhang, with one supporting post visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the full expanse of the wooden plank wall and the hip roof, with no doors or windows visible.
description_view_3: Back view showing a plain wooden wall and the symmetrical hip roof, with no openings or architectural features.
attribute_door_style: sliding barn door, wooden with black metal hinges
